request.intent: Crear presupuesto Michel Nory desde prompt estructurado GIA

document.notes: Ruta personalizada hacia Data Analyst & AI Engineering. Incluye acceso completo al itinerario Data Analyst & AI Engineering, Full Stack Sprint incluido, acceso a contenidos hasta diciembre de 2027, tutor personalizado, acompanamiento semana a semana, revision de proyectos, entregables y portfolio, soporte y orientacion durante el itinerario, proyecto final guiado, Hiring Project posterior personalizado, CV, LinkedIn, GitHub y portfolio. Objetivo: portfolio profesional y preparacion para primer empleo tech. Formacion/bootcamp. IGIC 0%. Crear solo la modalidad de 2.999,00 EUR. No crear presupuesto por modalidad alternativa de 1.599,00 EUR. Creado por GIA desde CRM + prompt de presupuestos. Fuente: Michel Nory · Ruta Data Analyst & AI Engineering — Reboot Academy.pdf. No enviar automaticamente al cliente.

info.title: Aikount
info.description:
Agent-first Spanish accounting SaaS — the API Holded should have.

All endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. Tokens are
either short-lived session JWTs (login flow) or long-lived API keys
prefixed with `agl_` (mint at `POST /api/v1/auth/api-keys`).

post.description:
Sign in or sign up with a Google ID token.

Three paths, in order:

1. ``google_sub`` already linked to a user → straight login.
2. Email matches an existing password-based user → silent auto-link iff
   Google says ``email_verified=true`` (otherwise we'd let anyone with
   any Google account hijack any aikount account by claiming its email).
   If the email is unverified, refuse and tell the user to log in with
   their password first.
3. No match anywhere → auto-create the user and send them to onboarding.

Returns the same ``LoginResponse`` shape as ``/auth/login`` so the
frontend doesn't need to branch.

get.description:
Return the authenticated user + active tenant + all memberships.

Tolerant of the pre-onboarding window: if the user has no tenant yet, we
return tenant=null and onboarding_required=true so the frontend can route
them to the wizard.

The "active" tenant is taken from the JWT's ``tid`` claim — that's what
switch-tenant updates. Falling back to the oldest membership (the
previous behaviour) made the TopBar switcher look frozen on the first
tenant no matter which JWT the client was holding.

Mark the user's email as verified and bounce them back to the frontend.

Always 302s — never returns JSON — because the user reaches this URL by
clicking a link in their mailbox, so the response has to be a browser
redirect.

post.description: Re-issue a session JWT pointing to a different tenant the user belongs to.

get.description:
Download a comprehensive JSON export of the user's personal data plus
the data of every tenant they belong to (GDPR art. 20).

post.description:
Erase the user account (GDPR art. 17).

Re-verifies the password, then deletes the user + memberships. Tenants
where the user is the sole owner are deleted with all their data (ON DELETE
CASCADE); tenants with other owners just lose this user's membership.

schema.title: Response List Members Api V1 Team Members Get
Response List Invitations Api V1 Team Invitations Get

post.description:
Create a new user + membership in one shot, without sending an email.

Returns the temp password — admin must capture it.

get.description:
Suggest the VAT tax type to apply on an invoice line for this contact.

``context=sale`` (default) classifies the *customer* (country + VAT + OSS
registration) the same way the 303/349/369 dashboard does and materialises
the matching ``TaxType`` so the invoice form can pre-fill the line tax (EU
B2B → IVAINTRA entrega intracom. exenta, EU B2C → OSS or IVA21, extra-EU →
EXPORT, domestic → IVA21).

``context=purchase`` classifies the *supplier* for a purchase line (EU B2B →
RCINTRACOM reverse charge, extra-UE → ISP/IMPORT, domestic → IVA21), reusing
the same logic as the AI purchase ingest. The user can always override it.

post.description:
Fold the ``duplicate_ids`` Contacts into ``keep_id``.

All Documents pointing at any duplicate are re-pointed to keep, then
the duplicates are deleted. Runs in a single transaction so an error
mid-flight leaves the DB consistent.

Backfills ``keep.vat`` / ``keep.country`` from the first duplicate
that has them when ``keep`` is missing those fields. We never
overwrite an existing non-null value on ``keep`` — same conservative
rule the AI ingest follows when adopting a VAT-less Contact.

Refuses to merge across tenants and refuses to include ``keep_id`` in
its own duplicate list (would delete the survivor).

Append the default-template widgets whose type isn't on the board yet.

Non-destructive: existing widgets keep their position; new ones are
stacked in fresh rows at the bottom. Lets users who already had a board
pull in newer widget types (e.g. "Ventas por país") without losing
their layout or recreating the board.

post.description:
Bulk-import historical sales invoices from a CSV/XLSX (e.g. a Holded
export). ``dry_run=true`` parses + maps + validates without writing, so the
UI can show a preview. Idempotent on the invoice number.

schema.title: Response Import Invoices Csv Endpoint Api V1 Invoices Import Csv Post

post.description:
Duplicate an invoice into a fresh draft (recurring-billing shortcut).

Copies the customer, lines, VAT and retention, but allocates a NEW invoice
number, dates it today and starts as a draft with no payments. Ideal for
clients billed the same concept every month: duplicate last month's invoice
and just issue it.

Generate a rectificativa (creditnote) from an issued invoice.

Clones the original's lines into a new draft ``creditnote`` linked back via
``corrected_doc_id`` and carrying the rectification reason (RD 1619/2012).
The result is a draft the user reviews and then issues, so the rectificativa
is always anchored to a real emitted invoice instead of being typed from
scratch and forgetting the link.

parameters.name: target

schema.description: Document type to create: 'invoice' or 'waybill'. Waybill is only allowed when converting an estimate.
schema.title: Target

parameters.description: Document type to create: 'invoice' or 'waybill'. Waybill is only allowed when converting an estimate.

get.description:
Render a Document (invoice or purchase) to PDF.

The template defaults to the tenant's ``invoice_template`` setting and can
be overridden by passing ``?template=classic|modern|minimal``.
Render a Document to standalone HTML (for iframe preview in the UI).

List bank reconciliations that link a movement to this document.

Used by the UI drawers (Compras/Ventas) to surface "this invoice was paid
with movement X on treasury Y" without needing a separate page.

schema.title: Response Document Reconciliations Api V1 Documents  Doc Id  Reconciliations Get

post.description:
Bulk-import received purchase/expense invoices from a CSV/XLSX. The CSV
"número" is the supplier's invoice number; dedup is per supplier on it.
``dry_run=true`` previews without writing. Supports an optional IRPF column.

post.description:
Auto-generate self-billed purchase invoices for outgoing bank movements
that need a supporting document (affiliate / contractor payouts where the
payee issues no invoice), in the same shape as the synced sales invoices,
and reconcile each against its movement. Idempotent: movements already
linked to a document are skipped.

schema.title: Response Autogen Purchases From Movements Api V1 Purchases Autogen From Movements Post

post.description:
Generate a rectificativa de compra (``purchaserefund``) from an issued
purchase — the purchase-side mirror of ``rectify_invoice``.

Clones the original purchase's lines into a new draft ``purchaserefund``
linked back via ``corrected_doc_id`` and carrying the rectification reason
(RD 1619/2012). **Amounts stay POSITIVE**: the journal engine inverts the
side for a ``purchaserefund`` (400 D / 6xx H / 472x H), so a supplier refund
must never be modelled as a negative purchase (which would post negative
amounts on the normal side and corrupt the libro mayor + IVA soportado).

The result is a draft the user reviews and then issues. For a **partial**
refund the user edits the line amounts / removes lines on the draft before
issuing (RD 1619/2012 rectificación por diferencias); a full refund keeps the
cloned amounts (rectificación por sustitución).

Contabilizar a draft purchase: move it to ``issued`` and post the
journal so it counts toward expenses / IVA soportado. A PDF is NOT
required — a manually-keyed purchase is booked the same as a PDF-ingested
one. Mirrors ``issue_invoice``.

Stream a ZIP with the rendered PDF for every selected sales document.

Each PDF is generated on the fly using the tenant's current template.
The ZIP name is unique-per-call so it doesn't collide if the user runs
several exports in a row.

Stream a ZIP with the original attached PDF for every selected purchase.

Purchases that have no attachment are skipped. The response surfaces a
``X-Aikount-Skipped`` header so the UI can warn the user about how many
were missing without us failing the whole call.

Delete the selected sales documents, undoing every side-effect.

Best-effort: a failure on one doc doesn't roll back the others. The
response lists every failure so the UI can show a clear partial-success
message.
Delete the selected purchase documents, undoing every side-effect.

Issue (draft → issued) every selected invoice that is still a draft.

Docs already past the draft state are reported as failures so the UI
can show what was skipped — we don't want a silent partial success.

get.description:

cross-bank manual matches and dropdown changes without refetching.

get.description:
Resumen agregado de TODOS los trimestres con docs del tenant.

Devuelve una fila por (año, trimestre) desde el primer doc hasta el
trimestre actual: ventas (base), IVA repercutido positivo, IVA
rectificado negativo (creditnotes), IVA soportado (compras), y el
importe a ingresar (output_neto - input). Útil para vista histórica
de cumplimiento fiscal.

schema.title: Response Aeat 303 Summary Api V1 Aeat 303 Summary Get
Response Aeat 349 Summary Api V1 Aeat 349 Summary Get
Response Aeat 369 Summary Api V1 Aeat 369 Summary Get

get.description:
Histórico OSS (369): una fila por (trimestre, país de consumo) desde el
primer documento hasta el trimestre actual — mismo patrón que
``/aeat/303/summary``. Solo incluye trimestres con ventas OSS (régimen
``eu_b2c_oss``), para no llenar la tabla de trimestres vacíos.

schema.title: Response Aeat 369 History Api V1 Aeat 369 History Get

get.description:
Modelo 303 en Excel (.xlsx): hojas de IVA repercutido, IVA soportado y
resumen del resultado a ingresar — el listado con formato que pidió Samu.

Modelo 369 (OSS) en Excel (.xlsx): resumen por país de consumo + detalle
por factura.

Return the list of providers the user can connect from the UI.

Unconfigured providers (missing platform credentials) are silently omitted,
so end-users never see ``set XYZ in .env`` style messages. The platform
owner configures providers globally; tenants see only what's offered.

The ``manual`` provider is always present — CSV import is built-in.

schema.title: Response List Providers Api V1 Integrations Providers Get
Response Stripe Connect Start Api V1 Integrations Stripe Connect Get

get.description:
List every Stripe gateway treasury connected for this tenant.

Multi-account: a tenant may hold several Stripe accounts side by side
(e.g. Upload-Post + Analiza Gráficas). Each shows its own balance, sync
status and a ``connected`` flag (true when the treasury has its own key,
false when it was created without one).

schema.title: Response Stripe Status Api V1 Integrations Stripe Status Get

post.description:
Connect (or rotate) a Stripe account via a user-pasted restricted key.

Multi-account safe: each (tenant, stripe_account_id) pair has its own
Treasury row that holds the key. Pasting a key for a NEW account creates a
new Treasury alongside any existing ones — nothing is overwritten. Pasting
a key for an already-connected account rotates that treasury's key in
place.

schema.title: Response Stripe Connect With Key Api V1 Integrations Stripe Connect With Key Post

post.description:
Clear the stored Stripe key. Treasuries themselves (and their movements)
are kept — only the key is wiped so the sync stops.

Multi-account: pass ``treasury_id`` to disconnect a single Stripe account.
Without it, every Stripe treasury for the tenant is disconnected (and the
legacy tenant-level key is cleared too).

schema.title: Response Stripe Disconnect Api V1 Integrations Stripe Disconnect Post

post.description:
Create (or return existing) a Treasury that mirrors the tenant's Stripe
Connect account as a virtual bank. Triggers an inline sync of the last 30
days of balance transactions so the UI shows movements immediately.

schema.title: Response Stripe Connect As Treasury Api V1 Integrations Stripe Connect As Treasury Post

post.description:
Kick off a full Stripe history sync in the background.

Returns immediately with ``{sync_status: 'syncing'}``. Poll
``GET /treasuries/{id}`` to read ``sync_status``, ``sync_progress`` and
``last_sync_error`` until status flips back to ``idle`` or ``error``.

schema.title: Response Stripe Sync Balance Api V1 Integrations Stripe Sync Balance  Treasury Id  Post

post.description:
Bulk-import paid Stripe invoices as sales Documents + Payments.

Body (all optional):
  treasury_id: UUID        - target a specific Stripe Connect treasury;
                             required for multi-account (Connect) tenants
                             since they don't have a tenant-level Stripe
                             key. Without it we fall back to the legacy
                             single-account mode.
  from_date: "YYYY-MM-DD"  - lower bound on Stripe invoice.created
  to_date:   "YYYY-MM-DD"  - upper bound on Stripe invoice.created
  limit:     int           - page size sent to Stripe (default 100, max 100)
  force:     bool          - re-import even if a Document already exists for the invoice

schema.title: Response Stripe Sync Invoices Api V1 Integrations Stripe Sync Invoices Post

post.description:
Import one Stripe customer's *entire* paid-invoice history on demand.

Powers the lazy backfill behind the customer-facing Invoices screen: when a
user (often just-subscribed) opens their invoices and we have nothing for
them yet, the caller hits this with their ``stripe_customer_id`` so the
legal invoices appear immediately instead of an empty page. Scoped to one
customer, so it's cheap enough for a synchronous read path, and idempotent
(dedupe by Stripe invoice id) so repeated calls never duplicate.

Body:
  treasury_id:        UUID  - the Stripe Connect treasury the customer lives
                              on (required; multi-account tenants have no
                              tenant-level key).
  stripe_customer_id: str   - the ``cus_…`` to import.

schema.title: Response Stripe Sync Customer Api V1 Integrations Stripe Sync Customer Post

post.description:
Crea facturas rectificativas (creditnote) para refunds/disputas Stripe
ya importados como BankMovement antes de que existiera la auto-generación.

Idempotente — saltarse las que ya tienen su rectificativa. Por defecto
procesa todas las Stripe gateway treasuries del tenant. Acota con
``from_date`` para no tocar trimestres ya presentados a Hacienda.

Body (todo opcional):
  treasury_id: UUID         - limitar a una sola treasury
  from_date:   "YYYY-MM-DD" - skip movimientos anteriores
  limit:       int          - tope por treasury (default 10000)

schema.title: Response Stripe Backfill Creditnotes Api V1 Integrations Stripe Backfill Creditnotes Post

delete.description:
Borra las facturas rectificativas auto-generadas por el backfill o el
sync. Filtro de seguridad estricto: SOLO Documents con
``external_source='stripe'`` Y ``external_id LIKE 'refund:%'`` —
imposible que toque facturas reales o manuales.

Body (opcional):
  from_date: "YYYY-MM-DD"  - solo borrar creditnotes con doc_date >=
  to_date:   "YYYY-MM-DD"  - solo borrar creditnotes con doc_date <=
  tenant_id se infiere del token; nunca acepta override.

Cascada: DELETE Documents → SQLAlchemy borra DocumentLine y Payment
asociados por la relación cascade='all, delete-orphan'.

schema.title: Response Stripe Rollback Creditnotes Api V1 Integrations Stripe Backfill Creditnotes Delete

post.description:
Re-clasifica las facturas Stripe UE B2C que se importaron como OSS
para que apliquen IVA 21% ES (régimen pre-OSS, Art. 70.Dos LIVA).

El TOTAL cobrado NO cambia (el cliente ya pagó esa cantidad). Solo se
recalcula el DESGLOSE interno con IVA incluido:
  base = total / 1.21    iva = total - base

Sólo toca facturas con ``contact_snapshot.regime == 'eu_b2c_oss'`` y
``external_source == 'stripe'``. Idempotente.

Body opcional:
  from_date / to_date  - acotar
  dry_run: bool        - si true, solo lista lo que cambiaría

schema.title: Response Stripe Reclassify Eu Invoices Api V1 Integrations Stripe Reclassify Eu Invoices Post

post.description:
Borra invoices Stripe importadas que no representan un cobro real.

Filtro estricto:
  - external_source = 'stripe'
  - doc_type = 'invoice'
  - NO tiene Payments asociados (subquery WHERE NOT EXISTS payments)
  - status = 'paid'

Son las prorations / ajustes de suscripción que Stripe netea contra
balance interno: status=paid pero amount_paid=0, así que
``_import_one_invoice`` no creó Payment. El cobro real ya está en otra
invoice (la siguiente del mismo subscription cycle) — borrar estas no
pierde dinero, solo elimina ruido contable.

Body (opcional):
  from_date / to_date  - acotar para no tocar trimestres ya presentados
  dry_run: bool        - si true (default false), solo lista lo que borraría

schema.title: Response Stripe Cleanup Zero Paid Invoices Api V1 Integrations Stripe Cleanup Zero Paid Invoices Post

post.description:
Import the tenant's Stripe customer base as Contacts.

Walks ``Customer.list`` (expanding ``tax_ids``) and upserts each row into
the ``contacts`` table. Existing contacts get refreshed in place — Stripe
is treated as the source of truth for the synced fields.

Body (all optional):
  limit:       int  - page size sent to Stripe (default 100, max 100)
  safety_cap:  int  - hard upper bound on rows iterated. With the default
                      ``200`` the call returns synchronously with the
                      counts of what got imported. Set to a larger value
                      and pass ``background=true`` to scan the full base
                      asynchronously (status is returned immediately).
  background:  bool - run asynchronously and return ``{"status":"started"}``.
                      The job updates the Stripe gateway treasury's
                      ``sync_status`` / ``sync_progress`` columns.

schema.title: Response Stripe Sync Customers Api V1 Integrations Stripe Sync Customers Post
Response Gocardless Status Api V1 Integrations Gocardless Status Get
Response Gocardless Banks Api V1 Integrations Gocardless Banks Get
Response Gocardless Connect Api V1 Integrations Gocardless Connect Post
Response Gocardless Sync Api V1 Integrations Gocardless Sync  Treasury Id  Post
Response Paypal Status Api V1 Integrations Paypal Status Get

post.description:
Connect a PayPal account via the tenant's own REST app credentials
(client_id + secret). Validates the credentials, stores them encrypted,
creates the PayPal treasury and kicks off a full historical sync in the
background. Mirrors the Stripe ``connect-with-key`` flow.

schema.title: Response Paypal Connect With Key Api V1 Integrations Paypal Connect With Key Post
Response Paypal Connect Start Api V1 Integrations Paypal Connect Get
Response Paypal Disconnect Api V1 Integrations Paypal Disconnect Post
Response Paypal Sync Api V1 Integrations Paypal Sync Post
Response Stripe Payouts Scan Api V1 Integrations Stripe Payouts Scan Post

post.description: If `payload` is provided, execute those exact candidates. Otherwise scan + execute.

schema.title: Response Stripe Payouts Execute Api V1 Integrations Stripe Payouts Execute Post

post.description:
Pure extraction — no DB writes. Returns the structured invoice payload.

Runs the extractor in a worker thread so the event loop stays free —
this endpoint is the legacy "preview" path; the real ingestion uses
``/ai/ingest-purchase`` which is fully async via the job queue.

Queue a single PDF / image for AI ingestion.

Returns IMMEDIATELY with the queued job — the actual extraction +
document creation happens in a background worker. Poll
``GET /ai/ingest-jobs/{id}`` or list ``GET /ai/ingest-jobs`` to track
progress; the row carries the same result payload the legacy
synchronous endpoint used to return inline (see ``IngestionJob.result``)
so the UI renders the same UploadResultCard once it lands.

Queue several files at once. Each ``files[]`` entry becomes one
job; if the entry is a ZIP, it's expanded and every PDF/image inside
becomes its own job. All jobs in the same call share a ``batch_id``
so the frontend can group their cards.

Returns the full list of created jobs (already queued, possibly mid-
processing by the time the response reaches the client).

get.description:
List ingestion jobs for the current tenant, newest first.

``active_only=true`` filters to ``queued`` + ``processing`` — handy for
the page-load fetch where we only want to rebuild in-flight cards.
Without the flag we return the recent history so the user can see what
was processed in this session (the frontend dismisses them when the
user clicks "ocultar").

Poll a single job. The frontend hits this every ~2s while a card is
in ``queued`` / ``processing`` state and stops when it transitions to
``done`` / ``error``.

delete.description:
Dismiss a finished job (``done`` or ``error``). The card disappears
from subsequent ``/ai/ingest-jobs`` listings.

Refuses to delete a row still in flight — the worker may be holding the
file open. If you really need to cancel, that's a separate feature.

schema.title: Response Ai Delete Ingest Job Api V1 Ai Ingest Jobs  Job Id  Delete

get.description:
Suggest the bank movement most likely to be the payment for this
purchase. By default INCLUDES already-reconciled movements (capped at
0.6 confidence) so the user can spot wrong existing links — pass
``include_reconciled=false`` to limit to pending.

schema.title: Response Ai Purchase Bank Match Api V1 Ai Purchases  Doc Id  Bank Match Get

get.description:
Suggest the bank movement most likely to be the customer payment for
this sales invoice. Mirrors the purchase variant; the only difference is
the sign of the candidate movement (positive credit instead of debit).

schema.title: Response Ai Invoice Bank Match Api V1 Ai Invoices  Doc Id  Bank Match Get

get.description:
Inverse of the two above: given a pending bank movement, suggest
the Document (purchase or invoice depending on sign) that most likely
explains it. Used by the reconciliation page's "Sugerencia IA" button.

schema.title: Response Ai Movement Document Match Api V1 Ai Bank Movements  Movement Id  Document Match Get
Response Ponto Status Api V1 Integrations Ponto Status Get

post.description:
Pull the tenant's Ponto accounts and create local Treasuries.

Body (optional): ``{"client_id": "...", "client_secret": "..."}``. When
provided, the credentials are persisted on the tenant (encrypted at rest)
before the import runs. If omitted, we use whatever the tenant has stored
— or, for the bootstrap admin tenant, the env-level fallback.

schema.title: Response Ponto Connect Api V1 Integrations Ponto Connect Post
Response Ponto Sync Api V1 Integrations Ponto Sync  Treasury Id  Post

post.description:
Generate a fresh SCA URL for the user to reauthorize the bank.
After completion, Ponto re-pulls the historical backfill.

schema.title: Response Ponto Reauthorize Api V1 Integrations Ponto Reauthorize  Treasury Id  Post

post.description: Force Ponto to query the bank NOW (separate from importing the data here).

schema.title: Response Ponto Refresh Api V1 Integrations Ponto Refresh  Treasury Id  Post
Response Ponto Connect Status Api V1 Integrations Ponto Connect Status Get
Response Ponto Connect Start Api V1 Integrations Ponto Connect Connect Get
Response Ponto Connect Sync Api V1 Integrations Ponto Connect Sync  Treasury Id  Post
Response Ponto Connect Disconnect Api V1 Integrations Ponto Connect Disconnect Post

post.description:
Disconnect ONE Ponto Connect account: revoke it at Ponto (stops the
~4 €/month) and keep its imported movements. If we can't revoke (e.g. the
consent expired), return 409 with the Ponto dashboard URL so the user can
finish there.

schema.title: Response Ponto Connect Disconnect Treasury Api V1 Integrations Ponto Connect Disconnect  Treasury Id  Post
Response Truelayer Status Api V1 Integrations Truelayer Status Get
Response Truelayer Connect Api V1 Integrations Truelayer Connect Get
Response Truelayer Sync Api V1 Integrations Truelayer Sync  Treasury Id  Post
Response Saltedge Status Api V1 Integrations Saltedge Status Get
Response Saltedge Providers Api V1 Integrations Saltedge Providers Get

get.description:
Create a Salt Edge Connect Session and return the hosted widget URL.
If `provider_code` is given, the widget skips the bank picker.

schema.title: Response Saltedge Connect Api V1 Integrations Saltedge Connect Get
Response Saltedge Sync Api V1 Integrations Saltedge Sync  Treasury Id  Post

post.description: Ask Salt Edge to re-fetch from the bank for this treasury's connection.

schema.title: Response Saltedge Refresh Api V1 Integrations Saltedge Refresh  Treasury Id  Post

post.description: Stripe billing events. Verified by signature; no auth dependency.

schema.title: Response Billing Webhook Api V1 Billing Webhook Post

get.description: Whether the agent is reachable. Never leaks env var names.

schema.title: Response Status Api V1 Ai Status Get

get.description:
List models available on the configured AI gateway, live.

Pass ``?refresh=true`` to bypass the 60-second cache. When the gateway is
unreachable (e.g. wrong API key, no balance) we return an empty list plus
the upstream ``error`` message so the UI can show why.

The response also carries ``provider`` (id/label/base_url) for the active
provider so the UI can render "Balrog · Qwen (self-hosted)" or "Vertex AI ·
Gemini" instead of a bare model list.

schema.title: Response Models Api V1 Ai Models Get

get.description:
AI spend for the tenant. Defaults to the current calendar month. Owner/
admin only — it's account-level cost information.

All changes that ever touched a single entity, newest first.

Pair entity_type with the SQLAlchemy table name (``documents``,
``contacts``, ``bank_movements``, etc.), not the model class name.

Global activity feed for the tenant.

The default view is "everything, newest first" — drop filters in via
query params to narrow down. Useful for the resident agent: it can ask
"what did I change today?" with
``GET /audit/activity?actor_type=api_key&from=2026-05-27T00:00:00Z``.

parameters.name: hide_system

schema.description: exclude actor_type='system' rows (cron syncs, OAuth refreshes)
schema.title: Hide System

parameters.description: exclude actor_type='system' rows (cron syncs, OAuth refreshes)

parameters.name: search

schema.description: substring match against entity_label
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: substring match against entity_label

get.description:
List request batches (groups of audit entries sharing a request_id),
newest first, each annotated with how many entries it contains.

This is the "undo last action" affordance: every batch is one HTTP
request, so reverting a batch undoes one user/agent action atomically.
Every audit entry belonging to one request, in chronological order.
Full snapshot + diff for a single entry.

post.description:
Apply one historical audit entry back onto the live data.

The restore itself becomes a new audit entry (operation=``restore``) so the
timeline records "this was put back" — which means restores are themselves
reversible.

Revert every change made by one request, atomically.

This is the "undo what the agent just did" button. The request_id comes
from any audit entry (``GET /audit/activity`` returns it). Reverts walk
the entries in reverse chronological order so dependent changes unwind
cleanly.

The revert itself runs under a fresh request_id so the user can see (and
if needed, un-revert) the recovery action in the activity feed.

schema.title: Response List Records Api V1 Verifactu Records Get
Response Record For Document Api V1 Verifactu Documents  Doc Id  Record Get

post.description:
Mint a public link for this invoice, or return the existing one.

Idempotent: if the invoice already has a token, we return it unchanged
so the user can copy the same URL again without breaking the link the
customer already received.

delete.description: Revoke the public link entirely. After this, the URL 404s.

post.description:
Force a new token, revoking any link previously shared.

Use when the customer's link has leaked (forwarded to the wrong person,
posted publicly, etc.). The old token immediately stops working.

get.description:
Return the issued invoices for a given contact, each with a signed
public URL ready to embed in the host app's customer-facing UI.

``code`` accepts either the AgentLedger Contact UUID or the Stripe
customer id (``cus_...``) — the latter is the recommended key for
integrations because it's already in the host's user record and is
stable across email changes.

Lazily mints ``public_token`` on any invoice that doesn't have one
yet, so the caller never gets back an entry without a working
``public_url``. The mint is idempotent across calls.

post.description:
Mint share-links for ``invoice_ids`` and email them in a single message.

Used by the "Enviar facturas por email" button on the customer view.
The destination defaults to the contact's stored email; the UI can
override it (e.g. the customer's billing email differs from the
primary contact email).

The endpoint also auto-mints tokens for invoices that don't have one
yet, so the user doesn't have to click "compartir" on each one before
sending.
Render a one-off invoice to PDF — same engine as the in-app invoices.

Render the invoice to HTML — used for the live preview (PDFs don't embed
reliably in an <iframe>; HTML always does, and it's faster).
Turn a free-text invoice description into structured fields via AI.

schema.title: Response Parse Natural Language Api V1 Public Invoice Generator Parse Post

post.description:
Derive an anonymized worked-example from a generated invoice and publish it.

The user's real invoice is never stored: we deterministically scrub all PII
and persist only the synthetic example + AI-written explainer. The dedup gate
(one row per scenario × sector) keeps anonymous traffic from spawning thin
near-duplicates — a repeat (scenario, sector) returns the existing page.

schema.title: Response Generate Example Api V1 Public Invoice Generator Example Post

get.description: Invoice as HTML for reliable in-page <iframe> embedding (PDFs don't).

confirm.title: Confirm
confirm.description: Must be true to proceed with erasure.

AccountDeleteRequest.title: AccountDeleteRequest
AccountDeleteRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /api/v1/account/delete`` (GDPR art. 17 erasure).

The password is re-verified server-side; ``confirm`` must be true so a
misfired request can't wipe an account.

AuditBatchSummary.title: AuditBatchSummary
AuditBatchSummary.description: Aggregated view of a single ``request_id`` group.

AuditEntryDetail.title: AuditEntryDetail
AuditEntryDetail.description: Full entry with snapshots and computed diff.

AuditEntrySummary.title: AuditEntrySummary
AuditEntrySummary.description:
Lightweight row used in list views — omits the full snapshots.

The full ``before``/``after`` JSON can be tens of kB per row (think
Document with 50 lines, dashboard layouts). The activity feed and timeline
don't need them — they're fetched on demand from
``GET /audit/{id}`` when the user opens an entry.

AutogenPurchasesRequest.title: AutogenPurchasesRequest
AutogenPurchasesRequest.description: Filter + target for auto-generating self-billed purchases from movements.

BankMatchHint.title: BankMatchHint
BankMatchHint.description:
The same suggestion shape the ingest flow already produces, plus a
convenience ``already_linked`` flag so the frontend can disable the
"aplicar" button when the doc already has its reconciliation.

BulkActionSummary.title: BulkActionSummary
BulkActionSummary.description: Per-id outcome of a bulk action — useful for partial failures.

BulkIdsRequest.title: BulkIdsRequest
BulkIdsRequest.description: Body shared by every bulk endpoint (download/delete).

ContactInvoiceSummary.title: ContactInvoiceSummary
ContactInvoiceSummary.description:
One row in the per-contact invoice list. Designed to be embedded
in a customer-facing application (e.g. Upload-Post's "My invoices"
page): every entry already carries a signed ``public_url`` the host
app can render as a direct link — no AgentLedger auth round-trip
needed from the customer's browser.

ContactInvoicesResponse.title: ContactInvoicesResponse
ContactInvoicesResponse.description:
Wrapper around the invoice list. Includes the contact identity we
matched on so the caller can sanity-check it picked the right customer
(especially useful when migrating between Stripe customer ids).

ContactStatsResponse.title: ContactStatsResponse
ContactStatsResponse.description: Aggregated sales/spend stats for a single contact.

CreateTenantRequest.title: CreateTenantRequest
CreateTenantRequest.description:
Spawn a brand-new tenant for the currently-authenticated user.

Used by the "Crear empresa" entry in the TopBar tenant switcher — lets
a gestor or owner manage several companies from the same login. The
full onboarding wizard's idempotency check would reject this (it
short-circuits when the user already has a membership), so we expose
a separate endpoint with a cut-down field set.

DocumentLineIn.title: DocumentLineIn
DocumentLineIn.description: Input for a line on create/update.

DocumentPaymentSource.title: DocumentPaymentSource
DocumentPaymentSource.description:
One treasury that paid (or partially paid) a document.

Used by list endpoints to surface "this sale was cobrada vía Stripe / PayPal /
bank account X" badges without forcing the client to fetch each document and
follow the Payment → Treasury join.

EmailInvoicesRequest.title: EmailInvoicesRequest
EmailInvoicesRequest.description:
Body for the bulk-send endpoint. ``invoice_ids`` are the documents we
want to share; ``to_email`` defaults to the contact's email when the
caller doesn't override it from the UI.

``cc`` and ``bcc`` are optional extra recipients. CC is visible in the
email headers; BCC is delivered as an envelope-only recipient — common
for the gestor or the user wanting a silent copy of every invoice email
sent to a customer.

ExtractPdfResponse.title: ExtractPdfResponse
ExtractPdfResponse.description: Pure-extraction response: the structured invoice payload, no DB writes.

GoogleLoginRequest.title: GoogleLoginRequest
GoogleLoginRequest.description:
The frontend sends the ID token returned by Google Identity Services.

We never see the access token or the Google client secret — we only
need the ID token (a JWT signed by Google) and verify it against Google's
public JWKS. The ``sub`` claim is the stable per-account identifier.

IngestJob.title: IngestJob
IngestJob.description:
Status payload for one queued/processing/done/error job. Returned by
the create / list / get endpoints — the frontend renders a card per
row and polls until ``status`` is terminal.

InvoiceShareResponse.title: InvoiceShareResponse

duplicate_ids.title: Duplicate Ids
duplicate_ids.description: Contact ids to fold into the path's keep_id.

PublicInvoiceView.title: PublicInvoiceView
PublicInvoiceView.description: Read-only invoice projection for the public viewer page.

QuickCreateRequest.title: QuickCreateRequest
QuickCreateRequest.description:
Create a user directly without sending an email invitation.

Use when the admin wants a "placeholder" user (e.g. an assistant who
doesn't have email but needs read-only access) or wants to share
credentials out-of-band. If `email` is omitted, a stable placeholder
address is generated under the `*.aikount.local` namespace.

accepted_terms.title: Accepted Terms
accepted_terms.description: Must be true: the user accepted the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.

SharedInvoiceSummary.title: SharedInvoiceSummary
SharedInvoiceSummary.description: One line in the bulk-email confirmation response.

SourceDocRef.title: SourceDocRef
SourceDocRef.description:
Resolved reference to a source document in the pre-sales chain.

Lets the UI render "Generado desde PRE-2026-0012" with a real, clickable
number instead of an opaque UUID. Only populated on full detail responses
(``DocumentResponse``); list endpoints stay light and expose the raw ids.

StatementImportBatch.title: StatementImportBatch
StatementImportBatch.description: One statement upload (extracto), grouping the movements it created.

SubscriptionCreateResponse.title: SubscriptionCreateResponse
SubscriptionCreateResponse.description:
Sibling of SubscriptionResponse that also reports how many existing
movements were linked to the new subscription as a side-effect.

TaxFilingUpdate.title: TaxFilingUpdate
TaxFilingUpdate.description:
Editable metadata. The attached file itself is immutable once uploaded —
upload a new document instead.

TenantMe.title: TenantMe
TenantMe.description: Full tenant payload returned by GET /tenants/me.

TenantUpdate.title: TenantUpdate
TenantUpdate.description:
Editable subset for PATCH /tenants/me.

Any field omitted is left untouched. `nif` is accepted as an alias of
`vat` for client convenience.

opening_balance.title: Opening Balance
opening_balance.description: Saldo inicial del extracto, anterior al primer movimiento importado. El saldo mostrado = opening_balance + suma de movimientos.

opening_balance.title: Opening Balance
opening_balance.description: Saldo inicial del extracto, anterior al primer movimiento importado. El saldo mostrado = opening_balance + suma de movimientos.

opening_balance.title: Opening Balance
opening_balance.description: Saldo inicial del extracto, anterior al primer movimiento importado. El saldo mostrado = opening_balance + suma de movimientos.

VerifactuSettings.title: VerifactuSettings
VerifactuSettings.description: Status payload for GET /verifactu/settings (never leaks secrets).

WidgetSpec.title: WidgetSpec
WidgetSpec.description: One widget in a dashboard layout.

interviewData.notes: Ruta personalizada hacia Data Analyst & AI Engineering. Incluye acceso completo al itinerario Data Analyst & AI Engineering, Full Stack Sprint incluido, acceso a contenidos hasta diciembre de 2027, tutor personalizado, acompanamiento semana a semana, revision de proyectos, entregables y portfolio, soporte y orientacion durante el itinerario, proyecto final guiado, Hiring Project posterior personalizado, CV, LinkedIn, GitHub y portfolio. Objetivo: portfolio profesional y preparacion para primer empleo tech. Formacion/bootcamp. IGIC 0%. Crear solo la modalidad de 2.999,00 EUR. No crear presupuesto por modalidad alternativa de 1.599,00 EUR. Creado por GIA desde CRM + prompt de presupuestos. Fuente: Michel Nory · Ruta Data Analyst & AI Engineering — Reboot Academy.pdf. No enviar automaticamente al cliente.
